data.title: PATEO CONNECT and Shanghai Jiao Tong University Forge OpenHarmony Embodied Intelligence Alliance
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An announcement from PATEO CONNECT Technology (Shanghai) Corporation Class H ( (HK:2889) ) is now available.

PATEO CONNECT Technology (Shanghai) Corporation has signed a strategic cooperative framework agreement with the Graduate School of AI Operating System of Shanghai Jiao Tong University to deepen collaboration in intelligent automobile technologies and embodied intelligence based on the OpenHarmony operating system. The partnership aims to combine PATEO’s industry position with SJTU’s research strength to accelerate innovation and build a self-reliant ecosystem for embodied intelligence.

Under the agreement, the parties will jointly pursue advanced academic research, co-host OpenHarmony embodied intelligence forums and seminars, and cultivate ecosystem-focused talent through special projects and open funds. They will also cooperate on setting industry standards, developing cloud–edge solutions for robots, smart cars and wearables, and leveraging their resources to commercialize OpenHarmony-based embodied intelligence products while seeking support from local government and technology authorities.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:2889) stock is a Buy with a HK$200.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on PATEO CONNECT Technology (Shanghai) Corporation Class H stock, see the HK:2889 Stock Forecast page.

More about PATEO CONNECT Technology (Shanghai) Corporation Class H

PATEO CONNECT Technology (Shanghai) Corporation is a China-based company operating in the intelligent automobile and connected vehicle technology industry. The Group focuses on in-car connectivity, smart operating systems and related computing technologies that support next-generation intelligent vehicles and broader embodied intelligence applications.

An update from Shanghai Zhida Technology Development Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:2650) ) is now available.

Shanghai Zhida Technology Development Co., Ltd. announced that its H shares have been selected as a constituent of the Hang Seng Composite Index, effective 9 March 2026, reflecting heightened market recognition of the company’s performance and value. The H shares will also become eligible for Southbound Trading under both the Shenzhen-Hong Kong and Shanghai-Hong Kong Stock Connect schemes from 10 April 2026, enabling mainland Chinese investors to directly trade the company’s Hong Kong-listed shares.

Management expects these inclusions to broaden Shanghai Zhida’s investor base, particularly among mainland institutional and retail participants, and to support higher trading liquidity in its H shares. The moves further integrate the company into key benchmark and cross-border trading frameworks, potentially improving its visibility in the capital markets and reinforcing its positioning within the Hong Kong–Mainland equity connectivity ecosystem.

More about Shanghai Zhida Technology Development Co., Ltd. Class H

Shanghai Zhida Technology Development Co., Ltd. is a joint stock company incorporated in the People’s Republic of China with limited liability and listed in Hong Kong as a Class H share issuer. It operates in the technology development sector, targeting capital markets access via its H shares on The Stock Exchange of Hong Kong to broaden its investor base and enhance trading liquidity.

data.title: SSY Group Wins NMPA Approvals for New Electrolyte and Antiemetic Injections
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

SSY Group ( (HK:2005) ) has provided an update.

SSY Group Limited has received approvals from China’s National Medical Products Administration for the production and registration of its Multiple Electrolytes, Sodium Acetate and Glucose Injection in 250ml and 500ml formats, as well as its Metoclopramide Hydrochloride Injection. Both products are classified as type 3 chemical drugs and have passed the country’s consistency evaluation, supporting their competitiveness in the regulated hospital market.

The electrolyte and glucose injection is indicated for replacing and maintaining water and electrolyte balance while providing energy when oral intake is not feasible, reinforcing SSY Group’s core infusion therapy portfolio. The Metoclopramide injection, used to prevent and treat various causes of nausea and vomiting and to assist certain diagnostic procedures, builds on the group’s earlier approval for its Metoclopramide bulk drug and is expected to broaden its injectable offerings and strengthen its position in China’s clinical treatment segment.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:2005) stock is a Hold with a HK$3.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on SSY Group stock, see the HK:2005 Stock Forecast page.

More about SSY Group

SSY Group Limited is a China-focused pharmaceutical group engaged in the development and production of intravenous infusion solutions and related injectable drugs. The company targets hospital and clinical markets with a portfolio of chemical drug preparations, including bulk drug ingredients and finished dosage forms approved by Chinese regulators.

Luks Group (Vietnam Holdings) Co ( (HK:0366) ) has issued an update.

Luks Group (Vietnam Holdings) Company Limited has convened its annual general meeting for 4 June 2026 in Hong Kong, where shareholders will review the audited financial statements for the year ended 31 December 2025 and consider a final dividend. The meeting will also address the re-election of one executive and three independent non-executive directors, the authorization of directors’ and auditors’ remuneration, the re-appointment of Ernst & Young as auditor, and the granting of general mandates for share repurchases and new share issuance, which together provide the board with flexibility in capital management and corporate governance.

These mandates, including permission to repurchase up to 10% of the company’s issued share capital and to issue additional shares or convertible securities within a defined period, underscore the company’s intent to actively manage its equity base. The resolutions, if approved, will reinforce the existing governance structure, maintain continuity in board oversight, and give management tools to support future financing or capital optimization initiatives, potentially affecting shareholder returns and market perception of the stock.

ASMPT Limited has convened its 2026 annual general meeting for 7 May 2026 in Hong Kong to seek shareholder approval of its audited financial statements for the year ended 31 December 2025, the reappointment of Deloitte Touche Tohmatsu as auditor, and key capital management measures. Shareholders will also vote on granting the directors a general mandate to repurchase up to 5% of the company’s issued share capital, and a separate mandate to issue, allot and deal in additional shares and related convertible instruments. These resolutions, if approved, would give the board enhanced flexibility over capital structure and funding options, balancing potential share buy-backs with future equity issuance capacity and signalling an active approach to capital management within Hong Kong listing and regulatory frameworks.

data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Infinity Logistics and Transport Ventures Limited ( (HK:1442) ) has issued an update.

Infinity Logistics and Transport Ventures Limited reported audited consolidated results for the year ended 31 December 2025, showing a slight decline in revenue to RM452.7 million from RM475.8 million and a modest drop in gross profit, reflecting a softer operating environment in its logistics and transport business. The group’s performance was further pressured by a sharp increase in loss allowances for receivables and higher administrative expenses, which, together with finance costs and tax charges, swung the company from a prior-year profit to a net loss attributable to shareholders, signaling near-term challenges for profitability and potentially heightened risk awareness among investors and other stakeholders.

Total comprehensive income also turned negative, weighed down by foreign exchange translation losses, underscoring the group’s exposure to currency movements across its operating markets. Basic and diluted earnings per share moved from positive territory to a small loss, highlighting the deterioration in shareholder returns and suggesting that management may face greater scrutiny over capital allocation, credit control, and cost management as the company seeks to stabilize results and protect its market position in the logistics sector.

Mexan ( (HK:0022) ) has provided an update.

Mexan Limited has announced the completion of a major share placing by its controlling shareholder, referred to as the Vendor, involving 1,326,735,354 existing shares, or about 67.47% of the company’s issued share capital. These shares were placed by a placing agent to at least six independent investors, each restricted from holding 30% or more of the company after the transaction, in line with Hong Kong takeover regulations.

Following completion of the placing on 9 April 2026, the Vendor ceased to hold any shares and is no longer the controlling shareholder of Mexan, marking a significant change in the company’s ownership structure. Two of the placees, investor Chen Jixiang and Max Premier Limited, which he ultimately owns, together acquired around 17.64% of Mexan’s shares and have become substantial shareholders, prompting the company to advise existing shareholders and potential investors to exercise caution when trading its stock.

data.title: Sino Biopharm Advances Kylo-11 With First U.S. Patient in Phase II Trial
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Sino Biopharmaceutical ( (HK:1177) ) has provided an update.

Sino Biopharmaceutical announced that its subsidiary Hangzhou Hygieia Biomedical has enrolled the first U.S. patient in a Phase II clinical trial of Kylo-11, an siRNA drug targeting the LPA gene for patients with atherosclerotic cardiovascular disease and elevated lipoprotein(a). The study, conducted in both China and the United States, will test dosing regimens of once every six months or once a year, positioning the therapy as a potential best-in-class, ultra-long-acting treatment for a major, currently unaddressed cardiovascular risk factor.

Kylo-11 uses a dual-conjugated siRNA delivery platform that has received global patent authorization and aims to deliver durable reductions in Lp(a) levels with favorable safety and tolerability. Early Phase I data showed robust and sustained lowering of Lp(a) across multiple dose cohorts with mostly mild, unrelated adverse events, underscoring the drug’s potential to fill a global therapeutic gap and strengthen Sino Biopharmaceutical’s presence in innovative cardiovascular therapies.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1177) stock is a Buy with a HK$8.70 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Sino Biopharmaceutical stock, see the HK:1177 Stock Forecast page.

More about Sino Biopharmaceutical

Sino Biopharmaceutical Limited is a China-based pharmaceutical group engaged in the research, development, manufacturing, and commercialization of innovative medicines. Through its wholly owned subsidiary Hangzhou Hygieia Biomedical, the company focuses on cutting-edge RNA interference therapies, targeting major unmet needs in cardiovascular and other chronic diseases in global markets.

The latest update is out from Tian Ge Interactive Holdings ( (HK:1980) ).

Tian Ge Interactive Holdings has disclosed that its current public float stands at about 20.85%, below the minimum level required under Hong Kong Listing Rules, largely due to ongoing share offer activities by major shareholder-affiliated parties. The company notes that a pre-conditional voluntary cash partial offer by Sina for 32,500,000 shares remains open after being extended, and full public shareholder acceptance would reduce the public float further to roughly 17.92%.

The board includes executive, non-executive and independent non-executive directors, and the company is subject to Hong Kong listing and securities regulations governing disclosure and inside information.

The company has formed a special committee of its independent non-executive directors to investigate an approximately RMB73 million transfer made in late February 2026 and its compliance with listing rules. An external independent accounting firm has been appointed as forensic investigator, and trading in the company’s H shares will remain suspended while the probe continues, prompting the company to urge shareholders and potential investors to exercise caution.

The special committee, chaired by independent director Hong Ting, will examine the background and impact of the transaction and report findings and recommendations to the board. ALSCO has committed to providing further announcements on the investigation’s progress and results, signaling ongoing uncertainty for stakeholders until regulatory and auditing concerns are resolved.

More about ALSCO Pooling Service Co., Ltd. Class H

ALSCO Pooling Service Co., Ltd. is a joint stock company incorporated in the People’s Republic of China and listed in Hong Kong under stock code 2649. The group operates through various subsidiaries, though this announcement does not specify its exact industry, products or services, and primarily addresses its status as a listed issuer subject to Hong Kong securities regulations.

The company’s governance and disclosure obligations place it under the purview of the Hong Kong Exchanges and Clearing Limited and The Stock Exchange of Hong Kong Limited. Its board comprises executive, non-executive and independent non-executive directors, reflecting a standard Hong Kong-listed corporate structure focused on regulatory compliance and investor oversight.

Jiashili Group Ltd ( (HK:1285) ) has provided an announcement.

Jiashili Group Limited has restructured key governance roles, with chief financial officer Liu Yun stepping down as company secretary and authorised representative as part of an internal reorganisation, while retaining her finance role. The board has re-appointed veteran governance professional Shoom Chin Wan, who previously served as company secretary from 2017 to early 2026, citing his deep familiarity with the group and extensive experience in compliance and corporate management.

Separately, executive director Tan Chaojun has retired from the board, a move the company says is amicable and acknowledged with formal thanks for his contributions. Following his resignation, Jiashili’s nine-member board now comprises six executive and three independent non-executive directors, bringing the company back into compliance with Hong Kong listing rules that require independent directors to account for at least one-third of the board.
